description: You are an orbital data coordinator providing a tracking dashboard for a thermal engineering team. GPredict is currently open with the default Amateur module (Map, Polar, and List views) and Metric units. Reconfigure the application for the thermal team by deleting the 'Amateur' module and creating a new module named 'Thermal_Monitor' that tracks the ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544) and CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274). The 'Thermal_Monitor' module must display only a List View containing the Name, Visibility (Vis), Orbit Number (Orbit), Altitude (Alt), and Footprint (Footp) columns, with antenna pointing columns (Az, El, Dir) removed. Set the global application preferences to use Imperial

natural_language.prompt: I need to set up an orbital dashboard for our thermal engineers. They need to track the ISS and the Chinese Space Station core module. The current GPredict setup has an 'Amateur' module with maps and radar that they don't want, and it's using metric units. Please delete the Amateur module and create a new one called 'Thermal_Monitor'. Add ISS ZARYA (25544) and CSS TIANHE (48274) to it. Change the layout so it only shows a table/list. In that table, remove the Az, El, and Dir columns because they don't care about antenna pointing. Instead, make sure Name, Vis (Eclipse State), Orbit, Alt, and Footp columns are showing. Finally, go into the preferences and switch the whole application to use Imperial units since their legacy docs use miles.
